titleStmt.title: Letter from Royal College of Physicians of Ireland to Ethel Benson, Dublin, 7 October 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Royal College of Physicians of Ireland

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
Letter from the Royal College of Physicians of Ireland (RCPI) to Ethel Martha Benson sending thanks for her donation of books to the college's library.
Ethel Martha Benson was the wife of surgeon Henry Arthur Benson (1852-1912). She was also the sister of Mrs Mary Lindsay, an elderly Protestant woman who was executed by the Irish Republican Army, alongside her driver James Clarke, in Cork in 1921. Mrs Lindsay's murder was one of the most controversial events of the War of Independence.

msIdentifier.repository: Royal College of Physicians of Ireland

correspAction.persName: Royal College of Physicians of Ireland
correspAction.placeName: Royal College of Physicians, Kildare Street, Dublin, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
7th OCTOBER 16. Mrs A.R. Benson,
Dublin. Madam, I am requested by the President and Fellows of the Royal
College of Physicians of Ireland to forward you a copy of the
following resolution which was adopted at a meeting of the
College held yesterday the 6th inst.:
Resolved:'that the President and Fellows of the Royal College
of Physicians of Ireland desire to return to Mrs Arthur Benson
their best thanks for the spendid donation of books which she
has made to the library of the College. The books are valuable
in themselves and are, on account of their association with Dr.
Arthur Benson who was so well known to, and honoured by, the
Fellows of this College. I have the honour to be, Madam,
Yours faithfully
